data.title: UK Treasury Roadmap Calls for Tokenization and Digital Money in Future Retail Payments
data.description:
UK regulators are urging tokenization and “new forms of digital money” to become part of the core infrastructure for the country’s future retail payment ecosystem. According to Cointelegraph, HM Treasury, on behalf of the Payments Vision Delivery Committee, said in a Thursday update to the government’s roadmap for modernizing retail payment systems that incorporating tokenization and digital money would support efforts to build a “diverse multi-money ecosystem.” The update also highlighted “programmable payments, including those that rely on tokenization,” as potential “product-level arrangements” that could help drive payment innovation in the UK. It further called for infrastructure that allows emerging forms of digital money to interact with traditional payment systems, reflecting an expansion of the National Payments Vision document published in November.

The policy direction comes alongside broader regulatory and operational initiatives affecting crypto and tokenized finance. Earlier this week, the UK’s Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) published its crypto regulatory framework and said the licensing window for crypto companies will open from September until Feb. 28, 2027, ahead of the regime going live on Oct. 25, 2027. Under the framework, cryptocurrency firms—including trading platforms, custodians, stablecoin issuers, staking companies, and other intermediaries—would need FCA authorization to operate in the UK. Separately, in April, the UK government said it would revisit its payments rulebook to support adoption of new payment technologies, including stablecoins and tokenization, and outlined plans for a consultation on reforms to payment services and electronic money rules to create a single framework for traditional and tokenized payments, including stablecoins and tokenized deposits, in an April 21 announcement by HM Treasury and Economic Secretary to the Treasury Lucy Rigby. In the following month, the Bank of England proposed extending operating hours for its core settlement infrastructure toward near-24/7 availability, citing support for cross-border payments and new payment and settlement models as tokenization develops; it is seeking public feedback until July 3 and plans to publish a feedback statement in the summer. The FCA also said days earlier that tokenization and distributed ledger technologies could improve fund management efficiency and support innovation in the UK asset management sector.

data.title: Anchorage Digital Integrates Lido to Offer Institutional Access to wstETH
data.description:
Crypto bank Anchorage Digital said it has integrated Lido, Ethereum’s largest liquid staking protocol, to provide institutional clients with direct access to the derivative asset wstETH. According to ChainCatcher, institutional users can mint and redeem wstETH through Anchorage while earning Ethereum staking yields.

Anchorage said the offering combines access to wstETH with its custody services and a governance and compliance framework for institutional clients.

data.title: Tesla Shares Trim Premarket Gains After Q2 Deliveries Beat Estimates
data.description: Tesla shares rose more than 2% in premarket trading before paring gains to under 1% after the company reported its second-quarter delivery results. According to ChainCatcher, citing a Jin10 report, Tesla posted total Q2 deliveries of 480,126 vehicles, exceeding market expectations.

data.title: Coinglass Data Show Hyperliquid Whale Holds $4.69B in Positions, Slightly Net Short
data.description:
A whale on Hyperliquid is holding $4.69 billion in total positions, with short exposure slightly exceeding longs, according to Coinglass data. According to ChainCatcher, the whale’s long positions total $2.28 billion, representing 48.48% of holdings, while short positions total $2.42 billion, representing 51.52%.

The data show the whale’s profit and loss stands at -$96.28 million on long positions and -$21.00 million on short positions.

ChainCatcher also reported that a whale address, 0x50b3..20, opened a 23x cross-margin short position on ETH at a price of $1,541.36. The position currently shows an unrealized loss of -$6.63 million.

data.title: Eric Balchunas Says June ETF Market Set Near-Record Issuance, Trading Volume, and Inflows
data.description:
Bloomberg senior ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said the ETF market delivered a “JUNE-SANITY” level performance in June, with multiple indicators nearing or setting records.

According to Odaily, data showed 214 new ETF products were launched in June, or about 10 per day, marking a record high. ETF trading turnover reached $7 trillion, the second-highest level on record.

The report also said net ETF inflows totaled $191 billion in June, the second-highest monthly level on record, averaging about $9 billion per day across roughly 2,700 different funds.

Balchunas said the figures indicate broad strength in ETF inflows, new product issuance, and trading activity.

data.title: AI | Microsoft Forms 6,000-Employee Group to Help Customers Deploy AI
data.description: Microsoft Corp. is setting up a new organization with 6,000 employees to help businesses with the technical and strategic work of deploying artificial intelligence, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Analyst Says Fed May Welcome Weaker-Than-Expected Nonfarm Payrolls Report
data.description:
Analyst Enda Curran said the nonfarm payrolls report may be a good outcome for the Federal Reserve despite coming in below expectations. According to Jin10, Curran said the labor market still showed improvement compared with last year.

He added that, with oil prices falling and signs that the job market is stabilizing, he believed the Federal Reserve would be satisfied with the result.

data.title: Ondo Finance Launches Third-Party Custodied Tokenized Securities Solution in the U.S.
data.description:
Ondo Finance said it has launched a third-party custodied tokenized securities solution in the United States designed to comply with local regulatory requirements. According to Odaily, the underlying assets are issued on Ethereum, and the company has partnered with financial infrastructure services provider Broadridge to provide shareholder governance and proxy voting services for holders of tokenized stocks.

Ondo Finance said the solution uses a two-layer compliance structure. The underlying shares remain held within a U.S.-licensed traditional custody system, while a registered transfer agent under Ondo mints corresponding tokens on a 1:1 basis and deploys them on the Ethereum public blockchain.

The company added that the underlying physical securities are simultaneously preserved by a compliant custodian.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Briefly Rises After June U.S. Payrolls Miss Expectations
data.description:
U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data showed non-farm payrolls rose by 57,000 in June, below market expectations of 110,000, while the prior two months’ payroll figures were revised down by 74,000. According to NS3.AI, the unemployment rate fell as the labor force participation rate declined.

Spot gold briefly surged following the data release. Markets also fully priced a Federal Reserve rate hike in December rather than October.

data.title: STOCKS | Blue Owl Capital Shares Rise 3% Pre-Market After Private Credit Fund Redemption Requests Ease
data.description: Blue Owl Capital shares rose 3% in pre-market trading after redemption requests for two of its private credit funds declined slightly from the previous period. According to Jin10, the decrease in redemption applications was described as a modest drop compared with the prior reporting period.

data.title: Community Reports Critical Alkanes Protocol Bug Allowing Unlimited $DIESEL Minting
data.description:
A serious bug in the Alkanes protocol has reportedly enabled unlimited token issuance, with one address allegedly creating 100 million $DIESEL using the exploit.

According to ChainCatcher, community user @CG_BRC20 said the newly minted $DIESEL could not be displayed on the protocol’s official Espo browser or on the Subfrost interface.

The report said users holding the asset should be aware of potential risks.

ChainCatcher added that an administrator in Alkanes’ official Telegram group has so far said funds are safe.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Falls to Two-Week Low of 100.58 After Nonfarm Payrolls Release
data.description: The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) fell to a two-week low of 100.58 after the nonfarm payrolls data was released. According to Jin10, the decline occurred following the publication of the nonfarm payrolls report.

data.title: Fed’s Daly Says Better Inflation and Labor Data Should Be Watched, but Goals Should Not Change
data.description:
Fed official Mary C. Daly said policymakers should pay attention to improved inflation and labor data, but should not change their goals.

According to Jin10, Daly said the focus should be on better inflation and labor-market readings while keeping the objectives unchanged.

data.title: U.S. Job Growth Slows as Unemployment Rate Comes in Below Expectations
data.description:
The latest data showed a combination of low job gains and a low unemployment rate.

According to Jin10, the unemployment rate was 4.2%, below broad expectations and below the 4.3% consensus among five major Wall Street investment banks.

The report showed 57,000 new jobs, ending a run of more than 100,000 new jobs over the previous three months. The figure was closer to Citigroup’s estimate of 25,000 and below the 90,000 to 130,000 range forecast by mainstream Wall Street investment banks.

data.title: US Hiring Slows Sharply, Curbing Recent Momentum in Job Growth
data.description: US hiring slowed sharply in June even as the unemployment rate fell, curbing some of the budding momentum in job growth this year, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: U.S. Short-Term Rate Futures Jump After Jobs Data as Fed Hike Bets Ease
data.description: U.S. short-term interest rate futures rose sharply after the release of nonfarm payrolls data, as markets reduced bets on further Federal Reserve rate hikes. According to Jin10, the market has fully priced in expectations for a Fed rate hike in December, after previously expecting a hike in October.

data.title: Nigel Farage Invests £215,000 in Stack BTC, Taking 5.61% Stake
data.description:
UK Reform Party leader Nigel Farage has invested £215,000 in bitcoin treasury company Stack BTC, taking a 5.61% stake.

According to Foresight News, The Guardian reported that Stack BTC’s investments have fallen 15.48% since the company was founded in March this year, resulting in a loss of about £565,000.

The report said Stack BTC’s largest shareholder is Paul Withers, who holds 20.72% and is also the owner of gold dealer Direct Bullion. Direct Bullion previously paid Farage £270,000 for 12 hours of brand promotion.

Farage denied serving as a brand ambassador for Stack BTC, saying he is only a regular shareholder. However, the report noted that he has appeared in a promotional video for the company.

data.title: Fed’s Daly Says Labor Market Has Stabilized as Inflation Rises From Tariffs and Oil Price Shock
data.description:
San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly said the labor market has stabilized, while inflation has risen due to shocks from tariffs and oil prices.

According to Odaily, Daly said oil prices have already retreated and she hopes related pressures will ease.

Daly added that Federal Reserve policy remains in a slightly restrictive stance.

data.title: Fed Rate Hold Seen At 68.5% For July Ahead Of Jobs Data
data.description:
The CME FedWatch tool put the probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in July at 68.5% ahead of the nonfarm payrolls release. According to Jin10, the tool showed a 31.5% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point rate hike by July.

For September, the tool put the probability of rates remaining unchanged at 35.7%, with a 49.2% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point hike and a 15.1% probability of a cumulative 50-basis-point hike.

For October, it showed a 27.8% probability of no change, a 46.2% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point hike, a 22.6% probability of a cumulative 50-basis-point hike, and a 3.3% probability of a cumulative 75-basis-point hike.

data.title: China A-Share Block Trades: Institutions Net Bought BOE A, Dongshan Precision, Star-Net; Net Sold Zhongke Feice, Fuchuang Precision, Shenzhen Kaifa on July 2
data.description: After-hours data showed that on July 2, institutions appeared on the Dragon and Tiger List for 103 A-share stocks, with 60 seeing net institutional buying and 43 seeing net selling, according to 36Kr. The top three net-bought names were BOE A (CNY 1.458 billion), Dongshan Precision (CNY 424 million) and Star-Net (CNY 293 million). The top three net-sold names were Zhongke Feice (CNY 2.506 billion), Fuchuang Precision (CNY 2.017 billion) and Shenzhen Kaifa (CNY 591 million).
